prompt.text:
Iterate on the EXISTING NocoBase CRM in env **expagents** — FULL AUTONOMY, no questions, end to end.

>>> nb CLI SYNTAX (READ THIS FIRST — getting it wrong wastes the whole run) <<<
`nb api` is organized by TOPIC, NOT by `<resource>:<action>`. `nb api collections:list` / `nb api workflows:list` / `nb api ai:listModels` DO NOT EXIST and return "command not found". Use the topic subcommands:
  - Read/write ANY collection's records:  `nb api resource list|get|create|update|destroy --resource <name> [--filter '<json>'] [--values '<json>'] -e expagents -y`
  - Collections/fields metadata:          `nb api data-modeling ...`
  - Workflows + nodes:                     `nb api workflow ...`  (subcommand `flow-nodes` for nodes)
  - Pages / blocks / fields / actions:     `nb api flow-surfaces ...`
ALWAYS pass `-e expagents -y` on every call. When a topic subcommand is missing a feature, fall back to raw curl against http://127.0.0.1:14230/api with the bearer token from `~/.nocobase/config.json` (envs.expagents.auth.accessToken).

CONTEXT — this beta.7 instance ALREADY has (DO NOT rebuild any of it):
- collections crm_customers / crm_contacts / crm_deals / crm_activities (seeded), with noun-named relations (customer/contact/deal/owner — never *_id).
- enums: crm_deals.stage = lead/qualified/proposal/negotiation/won/lost ; crm_customers.status = prospect/active/churned ; crm_activities.type = call/email/meeting/task ; crm_activities has a `done` checkbox + `due` date.
- 5 pages under the "CRM" menu group: Dashboard, Pipeline, Customers, Contacts, Activities.
- AI is ALREADY fully wired: an LLM service "qwen-zengo" (model qwen3.7-plus) is configured and all 8 built-in AI employees (atlas, dara, dex, ellis, lexi, nathan, vera, viz) are bound to it and ANSWER. You do NOT touch any LLM / aiEmployees model config — just surface the avatars (Part B).

Read these skills FIRST (opencode does not auto-load them):
- ~/.claude/skills/nocobase-workflow-manage/SKILL.md   (Part A)
- ~/.claude/skills/nocobase-ui-builder/SKILL.md        (Part B — editing deployed page blocks)

Do TWO things, in this order.

========================================================================
PART A — Add 3 CRM workflows (nb api workflow ...). Configure all nodes, ENABLE each, and TRIGGER-TEST each.
========================================================================
1. "Deal Won -> Activate & Onboard"
   - Trigger: collection event on crm_deals, AFTER UPDATE.  Condition: stage == "won".
   - Actions: (a) update the deal's related crm_customers row -> status = "active";
              (b) create a crm_activities row { type:"task", subject:"Kickoff & onboarding", deal:<the deal>, due: today+3 days, done:false }.
2. "New Deal -> First Follow-up"
   - Trigger: collection event on crm_deals, AFTER CREATE.
   - Action: create crm_activities { type:"call", subject:"Initial follow-up call", deal:<the deal>, contact:<the deal's contact>, due: today+1 day }.
3. "Deal Lost -> Post-mortem"
   - Trigger: collection event on crm_deals, AFTER UPDATE.  Condition: stage == "lost".
   - Action: create crm_activities { type:"task", subject:"Post-mortem: why deal was lost", deal:<the deal>, due: today+2 days }.

TEST (use `nb api resource update/create --resource crm_deals ...` then read back `nb api resource list --resource crm_activities --filter ...`):
- Set one existing deal stage="won" -> confirm its customer.status flipped to "active" AND a "Kickoff & onboarding" activity now exists for it.
- Create one new deal -> confirm an "Initial follow-up call" activity appeared.
Report exactly what you observed.

========================================================================
PART B — Surface AI employee avatars inside the CRM page blocks
========================================================================
The 8 employees already answer (LLM pre-wired). Put their avatars into the pages so users can chat in-context. Get LIVE page/block uids from `nb api flow-surfaces get ...` (+ desktopRoutes via `nb api resource list --resource desktopRoutes`). Edit deployed flowModels with `nb api flow-surfaces ...`; if a needed AI-employee model is not expressible through flow-surfaces, fall back to raw `flowModels:save` via curl (flat full-field upsert that PRESERVES parentId) — NEVER flowModels:update (it wipes parentId, the node vanishes). After each add, sync the containing block/page layout so it renders.

AI-employee FlowModel facts:
- Page-level avatar bar: `AIEmployeeShortcutListModel` is the page container (subKey "ai-shortcuts", subType "object"); each avatar inside is an `AIEmployeeShortcutModel` (subKey "shortcuts", subType "array") carrying the employee username.
- Block action-bar button: `AIEmployeeButtonModel` in a data block's actions (subKey "actions", subType "array"), carrying the employee username + a label.

Add:
1. Dashboard page: a page-level AI shortcut bar showing @atlas and @vera (top of page).
2. Customers page, the customers Table block: an AI button for @dara in the block action bar, label "Ask AI about this account".
3. Pipeline page, the deals block: an AI button for @dex, label "Draft follow-up".

========================================================================
FINAL REPORT (required)
========================================================================
- Part A: the 3 workflow keys + node count each + the exact trigger-test results (did the customer flip? which activity rows got created?).
- Part B: for each of the 3 placements, the flowModel uid you created + which page/block it sits in; confirm via `flow-surfaces get` that it is present in the schema.
- Call out any step where flow-surfaces could not express the AI-employee model and you had to use raw flowModels:save (this is the main thing we are testing — be specific about what failed and the workaround).

prompt.text:
Build a lightweight CRM in NocoBase, FULL AUTONOMY — no questions, do it end to end. Build into env **flash14231** — every nb api call uses `-e flash14231 -y`. The instance is a CLEAN empty NocoBase.

DATA MODEL — snake_case collections; belongsTo/relation fields named as NOUNS (e.g. `customer`, `contact`, `owner`), NEVER ending in `_id` (beta rejects `*_id` association names):
- crm_customers: name, industry (single-select enum), size (enum: SMB / Mid-Market / Enterprise), website, status (enum: prospect / active / churned), owner (belongsTo users)
- crm_contacts: name, email, phone, title, customer (belongsTo crm_customers), owner (belongsTo users)
- crm_deals: title, customer (belongsTo crm_customers), contact (belongsTo crm_contacts), stage (enum: lead / qualified / proposal / negotiation / won / lost), amount (number, ¥ prefix, 2 decimals), expected_close (date), owner (belongsTo users)
- crm_activities: subject, type (enum: call / email / meeting / task), due (date), done (checkbox), deal (belongsTo crm_deals), contact (belongsTo crm_contacts)

SEED all-English data: ≥8 customers, ≥12 contacts, ≥16 deals covering EVERY stage (incl. won & lost), ≥24 activities covering every type, with realistic owners/amounts/dates.

PAGES — use native NocoBase blocks only (Table / Kanban / Calendar / Details / Form / Chart). Put everything under a top-level "CRM" menu group. Plan per page:

1. Dashboard — a sales overview built from NATIVE Chart blocks (use the Chart builder block):
   - Deals count by stage (bar or funnel)
   - Pipeline amount by stage, or amount by expected_close month (bar/line)
   - Deals by owner (pie)
   - plus a Table block "Closing soon" (crm_deals sorted by expected_close ascending, stage not won/lost) and/or "Recent activities".
2. Pipeline — a Kanban of crm_deals grouped by `stage` (cards show title + customer + amount), AND a Table view of all deals with Filter + Add. Row/card click → drawer: deal Details + an Activities sub-table (that deal) + the linked contact.
3. Customers — Table + Filter + Add new. Row click → a WIDE drawer: customer Details + a Contacts sub-table + a Deals sub-table (both scoped to that customer).
4. Contacts — Table (with a Customer column) + Filter + Add. Row click → drawer: Details + an Activities sub-table.
5. Activities — a Calendar view (by `due`) AND a Table; Filter by type and by done; row click → drawer with Details.

Build it like a real product CRM a NocoBase consultant would ship — clean, populated, navigable. Sub-tables in drawers must auto-filter to the parent record. When done, report the page schemaUid(s) and a one-line summary of what each page contains.

outcome.finalText:
---

## CRM Build Complete — Final Report

**Environment**: `flash14231` (NocoBase 2.1.0-alpha.47)

### Data Model (4 collections)

| Collection | Fields | Relations |
|---|---|---|
| `crm_customers` | name, industry (select), size (select), website (url), status (select) | owner → users |
| `crm_contacts` | name, email, phone, title | customer → crm_customers, owner → users |
| `crm_deals` | title, stage (select), amount (number), expected_close (dateOnly) | customer → crm_customers, contact → crm_contacts, owner → users |
| `crm_activities` | subject, type (select), due (dateOnly), done (checkbox) | deal → crm_deals, contact → crm_contacts |

### Pages (all under "CRM" menu group)

| Page | pageSchemaUid | Contents |
|---|---|---|
| **Dashboard** | `zwow81nbwoh` | 3 native Chart blocks (Deals count by stage bar, Pipeline amount by stage bar, Deals by owner pie) + Closing Soon table (sorted by expected_close asc, excludes won/lost) + Recent Activities table |
| **Pipeline** | `fkvy26k4zja` | Kanban of deals grouped by stage (6 columns: lead→lost) + All Deals table with filter/add; row click → drawer with deal Details + Activities sub-table |
| **Customers** | `ibctvgdyrfv` | Table + Filter + Add; row click → wide drawer with customer Details + Contacts sub-table + Deals sub-table (both auto-filtered to parent) |
| **Contacts** | `xdp0utpyv3f` | Table (with Customer column) + Filter + Add; row click → drawer with Details + Activities sub-table |
| **Activities** | `3zkd8x0jt6q` | Calendar view (by `due` date) + Table; Filter by type/done; row click → drawer with Details |

### Seed Data

| Collection | Count | Distribution |
|---|---|---|
| Customers | 10 | status: active(5), prospect(4), churned(1); industry: 6 values; size: 3 values |
| Contacts | 14 | All linked to customers, realistic names/emails/phones |
| Deals | 18 | stage: lead(4), qualified(3), proposal(4), negotiation(2), won(3), lost(2); amounts ¥45K–¥500K |
| Activities | 26 | type: call(7), email(7), m

prompt.text:
Build a lightweight CRM in NocoBase, FULL AUTONOMY — no questions, do it end to end. Build into env **fable14232** — every nb api call uses `-e fable14232 -y`. The instance is a CLEAN empty NocoBase.

DATA MODEL — snake_case collections; belongsTo/relation fields named as NOUNS (e.g. `customer`, `contact`, `owner`), NEVER ending in `_id` (beta rejects `*_id` association names):
- crm_customers: name, industry (single-select enum), size (enum: SMB / Mid-Market / Enterprise), website, status (enum: prospect / active / churned), owner (belongsTo users)
- crm_contacts: name, email, phone, title, customer (belongsTo crm_customers), owner (belongsTo users)
- crm_deals: title, customer (belongsTo crm_customers), contact (belongsTo crm_contacts), stage (enum: lead / qualified / proposal / negotiation / won / lost), amount (number, ¥ prefix, 2 decimals), expected_close (date), owner (belongsTo users)
- crm_activities: subject, type (enum: call / email / meeting / task), due (date), done (checkbox), deal (belongsTo crm_deals), contact (belongsTo crm_contacts)

SEED all-English data: ≥8 customers, ≥12 contacts, ≥16 deals covering EVERY stage (incl. won & lost), ≥24 activities covering every type, with realistic owners/amounts/dates.

PAGES — use native NocoBase blocks only (Table / Kanban / Calendar / Details / Form / Chart). Put everything under a top-level "CRM" menu group. Plan per page:

1. Dashboard — a sales overview built from NATIVE Chart blocks (use the Chart builder block):
   - Deals count by stage (bar or funnel)
   - Pipeline amount by stage, or amount by expected_close month (bar/line)
   - Deals by owner (pie)
   - plus a Table block "Closing soon" (crm_deals sorted by expected_close ascending, stage not won/lost) and/or "Recent activities".
2. Pipeline — a Kanban of crm_deals grouped by `stage` (cards show title + customer + amount), AND a Table view of all deals with Filter + Add. Row/card click → drawer: deal Details + an Activities sub-table (that deal) + the linked contact.
3. Customers — Table + Filter + Add new. Row click → a WIDE drawer: customer Details + a Contacts sub-table + a Deals sub-table (both scoped to that customer).
4. Contacts — Table (with a Customer column) + Filter + Add. Row click → drawer: Details + an Activities sub-table.
5. Activities — a Calendar view (by `due`) AND a Table; Filter by type and by done; row click → drawer with Details.

Build it like a real product CRM a NocoBase consultant would ship — clean, populated, navigable. Sub-tables in drawers must auto-filter to the parent record. When done, report the page schemaUid(s) and a one-line summary of what each page contains.

outcome.finalText:
## Final Report — CRM on `fable14232`

### Data Model (4 collections)

| Collection | Fields | Relations |
|---|---|---|
| `crm_customers` | name, industry(select), size(select), website, status(select) | owner→users, ←contacts, ←deals |
| `crm_contacts` | name, email, phone, title | customer→crm_customers, owner→users, ←deals, ←activities |
| `crm_deals` | title, stage(select), amount(number), expected_close(dateOnly) | customer→crm_customers, contact→crm_contacts, owner→users, ←activities |
| `crm_activities` | subject, type(select), due(dateOnly), done(checkbox) | deal→crm_deals, contact→crm_contacts |

### Pages (all under "CRM" menu group)

| Page | pageSchemaUid | Summary |
|---|---|---|
| **Dashboard** | `yacui6jnjhg` | 3 native Chart blocks (Deals by Stage bar, Pipeline Amount by Stage bar, Deals by Owner pie) + "Closing Soon" table sorted by expected_close |
| **Pipeline** | `66bf7gmi3kj` | Tab 1: Kanban of deals grouped by stage (6 columns); Tab 2: Table with filter+add; row click → drawer with deal Details + Activities sub-table |
| **Customers** | `g7eq09tjbls` | Table with filter+add; row click → drawer with customer Details + Contacts sub-table + Deals sub-table (side-by-side) |
| **Contacts** | `i25me6nrtzc` | Table with filter+add; row click → drawer with contact Details + Activities sub-table |
| **Activities** | `sbkcmh43v7u` | Tab 1: Calendar view by due date; Tab 2: Table with filter+add; row click → drawer with activity Details |

### Data Counts

| Collection | Count | Enum Coverage |
|---|---|---|
| crm_customers | 8 | status: active(4), prospect(3), churned(1) |
| crm_contacts | 12 | All linked to customers |
| crm_deals | 16 | stage: lead(3), qualified(3), proposal(3), negotiation(2), won(3), lost(2) |
| crm_activities | 24 | type: call(6), email(6), meeting(6), task(6) |

### Known Gaps / Tradeoffs

- **Owner field**: Only 1 user (admin) exists; all records assigned to admin. Add more users for realistic owner distribution.
- **Chart "Closi

prompt.text:
Build a lightweight CRM in NocoBase, FULL AUTONOMY — no questions, do it end to end. Build into env **crmnative** — every nb api call uses `-e crmnative -y`. The instance is a CLEAN empty NocoBase.

DATA MODEL — snake_case collections; belongsTo/relation fields named as NOUNS (e.g. `customer`, `contact`, `owner`), NEVER ending in `_id` (beta rejects `*_id` association names):
- crm_customers: name, industry (single-select enum), size (enum: SMB / Mid-Market / Enterprise), website, status (enum: prospect / active / churned), owner (belongsTo users)
- crm_contacts: name, email, phone, title, customer (belongsTo crm_customers), owner (belongsTo users)
- crm_deals: title, customer (belongsTo crm_customers), contact (belongsTo crm_contacts), stage (enum: lead / qualified / proposal / negotiation / won / lost), amount (number, ¥ prefix, 2 decimals), expected_close (date), owner (belongsTo users)
- crm_activities: subject, type (enum: call / email / meeting / task), due (date), done (checkbox), deal (belongsTo crm_deals), contact (belongsTo crm_contacts)

SEED all-English data: ≥8 customers, ≥12 contacts, ≥16 deals covering EVERY stage (incl. won & lost), ≥24 activities covering every type, with realistic owners/amounts/dates.

PAGES — use native NocoBase blocks only (Table / Kanban / Calendar / Details / Form / Chart). Put everything under a top-level "CRM" menu group. Plan per page:

1. Dashboard — a sales overview built from NATIVE Chart blocks (use the Chart builder block):
   - Deals count by stage (bar or funnel)
   - Pipeline amount by stage, or amount by expected_close month (bar/line)
   - Deals by owner (pie)
   - plus a Table block "Closing soon" (crm_deals sorted by expected_close ascending, stage not won/lost) and/or "Recent activities".
2. Pipeline — a Kanban of crm_deals grouped by `stage` (cards show title + customer + amount), AND a Table view of all deals with Filter + Add. Row/card click → drawer: deal Details + an Activities sub-table (that deal) + the linked contact.
3. Customers — Table + Filter + Add new. Row click → a WIDE drawer: customer Details + a Contacts sub-table + a Deals sub-table (both scoped to that customer).
4. Contacts — Table (with a Customer column) + Filter + Add. Row click → drawer: Details + an Activities sub-table.
5. Activities — a Calendar view (by `due`) AND a Table; Filter by type and by done; row click → drawer with Details.

Build it like a real product CRM a NocoBase consultant would ship — clean, populated, navigable. Sub-tables in drawers must auto-filter to the parent record. When done, report the page schemaUid(s) and a one-line summary of what each page contains.

outcome.finalText:
CRM built end-to-end in env `crmnative`. Here's the summary:

**Data Model** (4 collections, 4 users):
- `crm_customers` — 10 records | `crm_contacts` — 14 | `crm_deals` — 18 | `crm_activities` — 26

**Pages** (all under CRM menu group):

| Page | schemaUid | Contents |
|------|-----------|----------|
| Dashboard | `f4n921c2hk8` | 3 Chart blocks (deals count by stage bar, pipeline amount by stage bar, deals by owner pie) + Closing Soon table + Recent Activities table |
| Pipeline | `xl219qxh4uv` | Kanban of deals grouped by stage (title + amount cards) + All Deals table with filter/add |
| Customers | `7zlg6qkg4px` | Table with filter/add; row click opens drawer with customer details + Contacts sub-table + Deals sub-table (auto-filtered to parent) |
| Contacts | `zl5snyjkotl` | Table with Customer column + filter/add; row click opens drawer with contact details + Activities sub-table |
| Activities | `6h6p8ckey1h` | Calendar view by `due` date + All Activities table with filter/add; row click opens drawer with details |

Build complete. Here's the summary:

## Page Schema UIDs

| Page | pageSchemaUid |
|---|---|
| **Overview (Dashboard)** | `3riwn5cq027` |
| **Products** | `lhmekv6v0fm` |
| **Suppliers** | `t8egup6d8qp` |
| **Stock Movements** | `dsh1yf88qmt` |
| **Purchase Orders** | `gd2lcknow8w` |

**Menu group:** "Stockpile HTML" (routeId: `372075254054912`)

## What was built

**5 collections** (all `h_` prefixed): `h_categories`, `h_suppliers`, `h_products`, `h_stock_movements`, `h_purchase_orders` — with m2o relations (`category`, `product`, `supplier`) using noun names.

**Seed data:** 6 categories, 5 suppliers (all 4 rating branches), 15 products (all 4 status branches), 26 stock movements (inbound + outbound), 7 purchase orders (all 4 status branches incl. cancelled). All English.

**Dashboard signature regions** (on page `3riwn5cq027`):
1. **KPI stat strip** — JSBlock: Stock Value, Total SKUs, Low Stock, Turnover Rate (4 cards, live from data)
2. **Stock Value Trend** — JSBlock: SVG area chart with gradient fill + dots + axis labels
3. **Value by Category** — JSBlock: SVG donut chart + legend with category breakdown
4. **Reorder Alerts** — JSBlock: 5 low-stock items with progress bars + severity colors
5. **Top Movers** — Native Table (h_products, 6 rows, with filter)
6. **Supplier On-Time Rate** — JSBlock: horizontal progress bars with rate %

outcome.finalText:
## Final Report — Fixed-Asset Management System (env: `maxbench02`)

### Data Model

| Collection | Template | Records | Title Field | Key Relations |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `asset_categories` | tree | 8 (3 root + 5 children) | `name` | self (parent/children) |
| `employees` | general | 4 | `name` | — |
| `assets` | general | 8 | `name` | `category` → asset_categories, `owner` → employees |
| `maintenance_records` | general | 6 | `title` | `asset` → assets |

### Enums Covered

| Field | Values |
|---|---|
| `assets.status` | `in_use` (4), `idle` (2), `under_maintenance` (1), `scrapped` (1) |
| `maintenance_records.maintenance_type` | `preventive` (2), `corrective` (2), `inspection` (1), `upgrade` (1) |

### Pages (all under **Fixed Assets** menu group)

| Page | pageSchemaUid | Blocks |
|---|---|---|
| **Asset Catalog** | `1n64v4xfi01` | Tree (left, category facet) + Table (right, asset list + JS depreciation bar column) |
| **Asset List** | `799wapr74w8` | Table (all fields, filter/addNew/view/edit/delete) |
| **Maintenance Records** | `3oy6ybvfpio` | Table (all fields, filter/addNew/view/edit/delete) |

### Known Gaps

- **Depreciation bar** is a standalone `jsColumn` (antd Progress) added via localized `add-field` after blueprint create — not part of the initial blueprint.
- **Card grid** was not used; the catalog uses a table with a JS-rendered depreciation column instead. NocoBase's `gridCard` block would require additional custom layout for the progress bar per card.
- **No detail drawer** with sub-tables (maintenance_records as sub-table under asset view) — standard ViewAction uses popup templates.

outcome.finalText:
## Final Report

### Page Schema UIDs

| Page | Schema UID | Menu Path |
|------|-------------|------------|
| Calendar | `1w5bh9piw3i` | Content Calendar / Calendar |
| Campaigns | `s7joej5f9bp` | Content Calendar / Campaigns |

### Collections

| Collection | Template | Fields | Records |
|-----------|----------|--------|---------|
| `campaigns` | general | name, description, status(select), start_date, end_date, budget + content_items(hasMany) | 4 |
| `content_items` | calendar | title, channel(select), status(select), startAt, endAt, description, author, campaign(m2o) | 15 |

### Enums Covered

- **campaigns.status**: draft(1), active(1), completed(1), archived(1)
- **content_items.channel**: blog(3), social(3), email(3), video(3), podcast(3)
- **content_items.status**: draft(4), scheduled(4), published(4), archived(2)

### Pages

1. **Calendar** — Calendar block (content_items, color by channel) + "This Month" summary table side-by-side, filterable by title/status/channel/description
2. **Campaigns** — Table with filter/addNew/view/edit/delete; view drawer shows campaign details + content_items sub-table (via `associationName=campaigns.content_items`)

### Design Decisions

- Relation field named `campaign` (not `campaign_id`) per task requirement
- Calendar uses `startAt`/`endAt` as date fields, `channel` as color field
- Summary table beside calendar shows all content items with filter capability
- Menu group "Content Calendar" with two pages underneath

### Known Gaps

- Not all channel×status combinations are populated (e.g., blog/archived, email/draft) — every individual enum value has ≥1 row but not every cross-product
- No workflow, ACL, or i18n configured (out of scope)

## Content Calendar App Built Successfully

**Page Schema UIDs:**
- **Campaigns**: `4tv3ov482ei`
- **Channels**: `ofzdq0uglrn`
- **Content Calendar**: `j3gsvil2ljd`

**Collections Created:**
- `campaigns` - 4 status branches (draft, active, completed, on_hold)
- `channels` - 6 type branches (social, email, web, print, mobile, event)
- `content_items` - 6 contentType branches (article, video, image, infographic, podcast, blog) + 5 status branches (idea, draft, scheduled, published, archived)

**Relations:**
- `content_items.campaign` → belongsTo → campaigns
- `content_items.channel` → belongsTo → channels
- `campaigns.contentItems` → hasMany → content_items
- `channels.contentItems` → hasMany → content_items

**UI Pages:**
1. **Campaigns** - Table view with filter, add new, view/edit/delete actions
2. **Channels** - Table view with filter, add new, view/edit/delete actions
3. **Content Calendar** - Calendar view showing content items by scheduled date + "This Month's Content" list with status/contentType filters

All pages organized under "Content Management" menu group with native NocoBase blocks (Table, List, Calendar).

outcome.finalText:
## Final Report — Inventory Management System (Complete)

### Data Model

| Collection | Type | Fields | Relations |
|------------|------|--------|-----------|
| `categories` | tree | name, description | parent (m2o), children (o2m) |
| `items` | general | name, sku, description, unit, current_stock, min_stock, status | category (m2o → categories), stock_movements (o2m) |
| `stock_movements` | general | movement_type, quantity, reference_number, notes, movement_date | item (m2o → items) |

**Enums**:
- `items.status`: active (green), discontinued (default), out_of_stock (red)
- `stock_movements.movement_type`: stock_in (green), stock_out (red)

**Relation naming**: All belongsTo fields use noun names (`category`, `item`) — no `_id` suffix on association names. Foreign keys use `categoryId`, `itemId` internally.

### Pages

| Page | Schema UID | Blocks | Menu Path |
|------|-----------|--------|-----------|
| Dashboard | `b4ozctns9qe` | Table (items with all fields) | Inventory > Dashboard |
| Categories | `zrgqwd5zs1k` | Table (tree) | Inventory > Categories |
| Items | `86tz7jyekr3` | Table + filter (name, sku, status) | Inventory > Items |
| Stock Movements | `2brq2rgfdof` | Table + filter | Inventory > Stock Movements |

All pages include View/Edit/Delete record actions with drawer popups. Relation fields have association popups configured.

### Test Data

| Collection | Rows | Enum Coverage |
|------------|------|---------------|
| categories | 5 | Electronics, Office Supplies, Furniture, Software, Networking |
| items | 8 | active: 5, discontinued: 1, out_of_stock: 2 — all 3 branches covered |
| stock_movements | 9 | stock_in: 5, stock_out: 4 — both branches covered |

**Relation completeness**: 8/8 items have category, 9/9 stock_movements have item.

### Known Gaps / Design Trade-offs ⚠️

**Trade-offs**:
- Details use drawer popups (not standalone pages) — enables `ctx.view.inputArgs.filterByTk` for auto-filtering sub-tables
- Categories use `tree` template —

outcome.finalText:
# 复刻完成 — 38 离职流程(Exit Checklist / Offboard)

按 `nocobase-prototype-repro` skill 全流程(读 SKILL.md + 全部 references → curl 原型 → 解析内嵌 nb-spec 为唯一事实源 → 建模 → 种子 → 原生 CRUD 页 → 招牌页区域细化 → 截图↔原型视觉环 3 轮)在 `fable14232` 端到端搭完,零 console/pageerror。

## Collection 清单(均 ext_ 前缀,显式 snake_case FK)

| Collection | 字段要点 | 关系 | 种子 |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ext_departures` | employee_name(title)、department(7枚举)、exit_type(5枚举)、status(4枚举)、LWD/面谈时间、reason_note | ← stages/tasks/assets 反向 o2m | 7 人 |
| `ext_stages` | name、key(5枚举)、sub、when_label、sort、icon | m2o departure(fk `departure_id`) | 35 行(7×5) |
| `ext_tasks` | name、group(5枚举)、owner、due_label、due_state(4枚举)、done、note | m2o stage + departure | 182 行(7×26) |
| `ext_assets` | name、category(8枚举)、asset_tag、condition(5枚举)、return_state(4枚举)、value_usd | m2o departure | 56 行(7×8) |

**英文种子覆盖全部枚举各≥1 行**:原型 3 人(Marcus/Sofia/Arjun)按原型进度精确复刻,另加 4 人(Elena 退休/已完成、Tom 辞退/damaged+missing 资产、Grace people_ops、Noah marketing)补齐 retirement/involuntary/completed/finance/people_ops/it/marketing/key/damaged/missing 等分支。

## 页面

- **招牌页 Offboard:pageUid `otzlm7e0emb`**(路由组 Offboard,routeId 369158739591168)
- 列表页(Table+Filter+AddNew+View/Edit drawer):Departures `tqzqk1fxdsl` · Exit Stages `nxw9655x2a1` · Exit Tasks `l4rtr6fwhbu` · Issued Assets `sbo78r2pqtm`

招牌页 = 单行三列 grid(5/12/7),6 个**原生 List 容器 + 行内 JSField**(无整页 JS 块):左侧切换栏(头像+exit chip+LWD+迷你完成环)、hero(渐变 cover+大头像+标签+3 个 quick stat)、大完成环卡、五阶段旅程时间线(图标节点、按 HR/IT/Admin/Finance/Manager 分组的勾选清单、负责人+due pill、阶段进度条、100% 自动折叠)、资产回收(汇总条+图标卡+Mark returned 按钮)、离职摘要(含全清绿色 banner 逻辑)。

**交互全部实测通过**:点左栏切人 → hero/环/旅程/资产/摘要全部重取数并高亮跟随;勾选任务写库(已验证 DB 持久化并还原种子)、完成环/迷你环/进度条联动刷新;Mark returned 同链路。

## Self-Score: 8.5/10

### 坑记录（本次新增）

1. **`ctx.makeResource` inside `useEffect` 被拒**：后端要求在 `ctx.render(...)` 之前加载数据；改用 `ctx.initResource` + 在顶层读 `ctx.resource.data.data`，再作为 props 传入组件。

2. **`flowModels:save` 写 JSFieldModel 必须带完整 `stepParams`**：代码存在 `stepParams.jsSettings.runJs.code`，不是 `props.code`；仅发 `{uid, code}` 不生效，须附上 `stepParams.jsSettings + fieldSettings`。

3. **Blueprint "key already defined" 400**：首次 apply 部分成功后再试报冲突；需先 `desktopRoutes:destroy` 清理孤立页面，再重新 create。

4. **`collectionMetadata` 需解包 `data` 层**：`nb api data-modeling collections get` 返回 `{data:{...}}`，prepare-write helper 需要解包后的对象；否则 defaultFilter 字段验证全报 "unsupported"。

5. **列表分页选择器**：注入 CSS 隐藏分页应同时覆盖 `.ant-list-pagination` 和 `.nb-list-pagination`，后者先被加载时

The module is built. Here's a summary of what was completed:

**Collections created:**
- `ast_assets` — 11 fields including formula `book_value`, attachment `photo`, `warranty_expiry`, plus m2m `tags` and hasMany `maintenance_records`

**Pages under "Asset Management" menu group:**
- **Assets** — Table with all 8 fields, Filter + AddNew actions, name-click opens a drawer containing a Details block + Maintenance Records sub-table (associatedRecords). Record actions: Delete, Send to Repair (→status=under_repair), Scrap (→status=scrapped). View/Edit removed per spec.
- **Maintenance Records** — Table with all fields including asset relation (titleField=name), Filter + AddNew actions, View/Edit/Delete record actions.
- **Maintenance Calendar** — Calendar block bound to `ast_maintenance_records`, start/end=maintenance_date, colorField=type.
- **Asset Overview** — JS KPI block (Total assets / Total value / Under repair), pie chart (Assets by Status), bar chart (Value by Category).

**Workflow:** Collection trigger on `ast_maintenance_records` afterCreate with condition `type==repair`, update node sets `ast_assets.status=under_repair`.

**Seed data:** 4 assets (all categories/statuses), 8 maintenance records (all types), 3 tags.

**Self-assessment: ~72%** — the Asset Catalog page (complex GridCard + facet JS filter) and some display styling were not implemented. See `REPORT.md` for full details.

The Content Calendar module is fully built on the `expagents` instance. Here's a summary of what was done:

**Data Model (3 collections):**
- `cc_campaigns` — 6 business fields + status select (4 options)
- `cc_contents` — 8 business fields + status/channel selects, cover attachment, body richText
- `cc_tags` — name field; m2m linked to cc_contents through `cc_contents_tags`
- M2O relation: cc_contents.campaign → cc_campaigns + reverse o2m cc_campaigns.contents

**5 Pages under "Content Calendar" group:**
1. **Campaigns** — Table with View/Edit + Filter/AddNew; View drawer has Details block + Contents sub-table
2. **Contents** — Table with campaign relation column, 3 updateRecord inline actions (Submit for Review / Schedule / Publish)
3. **Editorial Calendar** — Calendar block (cc_contents, titleField=title, colorField=status, startField/endField=publish_date)
4. **Content Kanban** — Kanban grouped by status, cards show title + channel
5. **Content Overview** — Bar chart (by channel) + Pie chart (by status) on row 1, Line chart (by month) on row 2

**Seed Data:** 4 campaigns covering all statuses, 10 contents covering all 5 channels × 5 statuses, 6 tags (2 per content), publish_dates spread across 2025-2026

**Workflow:** "Auto Fill Publish Date" — collection trigger on cc_contents update when status=published, sets publish_date=$system.now if empty. Enabled.

# FINAL REPORT — OSS Template #65 "审批工作流系统 / Approval Workflow System" → NocoBase `flash14231`

## ① Collections (prefix `app65_`, all created in one modeling pass)
| Collection | Title | Key fields | Relations |
|---|---|---|---|
| **app65_requests** | Approval Requests | req_code, title, type(7-enum), requester, dept, amount, currency, submitted, age_hours, urgent/watching/my_stage(bool), risk(low/med/high), ai_score, ai_class, sla, status(pending/review/escalated/approved/rejected), reason, suggestion | `steps` o2m, `extracts` o2m |
| **app65_steps** | Approval Steps | seq, role, who, state(auto/done/current/pending/rejected), note, step_time | `request` m2o (fk requestId) |
| **app65_extracts** | AI Extractions | label, value, confidence(hi/md) | `request` m2o (fk requestId) |
| **app65_feed** | AI Triage Feed | feed_color(violet/green/amber/blue), message, time_ago, req_ref | — |

**Seed (all-English, every enum ≥1 row):** 9 requests covering all 7 types + all 5 statuses (incl. added `approved`/`rejected` rows), 34 steps (all 5 states), 36 extractions (hi+md), 7 feed items (all 4 colors).
